properties.name: Marwar Region
properties.geometryNote: Boundary is the union of the constituent districts. The classification's actual boundary crosses districts; refer to the cited source for the authoritative line.

facts: Rajasthan's largest cultural region by area — covers the entire central and western Thar.
Includes Godwar as a distinct sub-region at the Aravalli piedmont.
Marwari dialect is the most-spoken Rajasthani dialect.

properties.core: Historical Marwar kingdom of the Rathore Rajputs
properties.physical: Arid + semi-arid Thar; Aravalli piedmont at the eastern margin
properties.economy: Wool + salt + handicraft exports + emerging petroleum

properties.name: Mewar Region
properties.geometryNote: Boundary is the union of the constituent districts. The classification's actual boundary crosses districts; refer to the cited source for the authoritative line.

facts: Anchored on Udaipur, Rajasthan's tourism capital.
Home to India's largest zinc-lead smelting industry (HZL).
Mewar Rajputs never accepted Mughal suzerainty — a defining historical thread.

properties.core: Historical Mewar kingdom of the Sisodia Rajputs
properties.physical: Southern Aravalli, forested hills, lake basins

properties.name: Hadoti Region
properties.geometryNote: Boundary is the union of the constituent districts. The classification's actual boundary crosses districts; refer to the cited source for the authoritative line.

facts: Rajasthan's wheat-and-soybean granary — vertisol-based agriculture on the Hadoti plateau.
Bundi's miniature-painting tradition and Kota Doria weaving are GI-registered crafts.
Coaching education has become the region's second industry.

properties.core: Historical Hadoti kingdom of the Hada Chauhan Rajputs
properties.dialect: Hadoti (a Rajasthani dialect close to Braj)
properties.physical: Vindhyan plateau + Chambal gorge; the wettest belt of Rajasthan

properties.name: Shekhawati Region
properties.geometryNote: Boundary is the union of the constituent districts. The classification's actual boundary crosses districts; refer to the cited source for the authoritative line.

facts: Home to the Marwari trading diaspora — the Birla, Bajaj, Poddar, Goenka and Jhunjhunwala families all originate here.
Fresco-painted havelis of Nawalgarh, Mandawa and Fatehpur are the world's largest open-air fresco gallery.
Highest sex ratio + high literacy among Rajasthan's northern belt districts.

properties.dialect: Shekhawati (a variant of Marwari with Braj influence)
properties.physical: Arid to semi-arid; sandy plains north of the Aravalli
properties.economy: Groundnut + mustard agriculture + emerging wind-solar power

properties.name: Dhundhar Region
properties.geometryNote: Boundary is the union of the constituent districts. The classification's actual boundary crosses districts; refer to the cited source for the authoritative line.

facts: Kachwaha Rajputs of Amber were Mughal allies — a strategic political choice that made Jaipur India's planned city.
Dhundhar hosts India's only planned pre-modern city (Jaipur, 1727) — a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
Jaipur is Rajasthan's Class-X metropolitan area — the only district with >50 % urbanisation.

properties.core: Historical Dhundhar kingdom of the Kachwaha Rajputs
properties.economy: Jaipur handicrafts + gems & jewellery + IT/ITeS + smart city

properties.name: Vagad Region
properties.geometryNote: Boundary is the union of the constituent districts. The classification's actual boundary crosses districts; refer to the cited source for the authoritative line.

facts: Rajasthan's Scheduled-Area belt under the Fifth Schedule of the Constitution.
Sex ratios exceed 990 F/1000M — matriarchal tribal demographics.
The Beneshwar tribal fair at the Mahi-Som-Jakham confluence is one of India's largest tribal gatherings.

properties.core: Vagad kingdom of the Bhil-majority southern Aravalli
properties.physical: Southern Aravalli + Mahi basin; the wettest, most-forested part of Rajasthan
properties.economy: Tribal agriculture + maize + hydel power (MBS) + emerging tourism

properties.name: Matsya Region
properties.geometryNote: Boundary is the union of the constituent districts. The classification's actual boundary crosses districts; refer to the cited source for the authoritative line.

facts: One of the ancient 16 mahajanapadas mentioned in the Anguttara Nikaya.
Matsya evolved as a Jat + Meo cultural mosaic — distinct from the pure Rajput identity elsewhere.
Alwar became the state name for a brief post-Independence union (Matsya Union, 1948).

properties.core: Ancient Matsya mahajanapada — one of the 16 great kingdoms
properties.physical: Aravalli extension into Alwar; eastern alluvial plain
properties.economy: Auto components (DMIC KBN) + mustard agriculture + tourism

properties.name: Godwar Region
properties.geometryNote: Boundary is the union of the constituent districts. The classification's actual boundary crosses districts; refer to the cited source for the authoritative line.

facts: A named sub-region of Marwar — often overlooked in atlases but culturally distinct.
Ranakpur's Chaumukha Jain temple is one of India's most important Jain pilgrimage sites.
Jawai reservoir is Rajasthan's celebrated leopard habitat.

properties.core: Sub-region of Marwar at the Aravalli piedmont
properties.physical: Aravalli western piedmont — a transition between the Thar and the Aravalli main axis

properties.name: Mewat Region
properties.geometryNote: Boundary is the union of the constituent districts. The classification's actual boundary crosses districts; refer to the cited source for the authoritative line.

facts: A cross-state cultural region — Rajasthan hosts only the southwestern part; the bulk lies in Haryana (Nuh, Palwal).
The Meo community traces its identity to a pre-Mughal conversion.
The state's only Muslim-majority sub-region within a Hindu-majority state.

properties.core: Cross-border cultural region shared with Haryana
properties.physical: Aravalli northeastern piedmont; extends into Haryana
